rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., DBA FIRST NATIONAL VS. THE SHEMANO GROUP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2009, line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFIC CAPITAL BANK N.A., AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for declaratory relief. The issues of the parties' liabilities under the control agreement are directly addressed in the complaint, thus the declaratory relief action is duplicative. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligence. Cross-complainant to plead the relationship between the parties that gives rise to a duty from cross-defendant to cross-complainant. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA A FISHER-KOTLAR et al VS. NOVARTIS PHARMACEUTICALS CORPORATION et al

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRE PUPO GOUVEA et al VS. CLARINDO GOMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT CLARINDO GOMES, and, BLACK HAWK CAR SERVICE LIMOS & BUSSES, LLC Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Motion for preliminary injunction denied. Defendants fail to show a reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits or that the balance of harm weighs in favor of issuing the injunction. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HARLEIGH REILLY VS. JP MORGAN CHASE & CO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT COLDWELL BANKER, VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC., ROBERT KINOSHITA, and, REALOGY CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to respond. A prelitigation communication is privileged only when it relates to litigation that is contemplated in good faith and under serious consideration. (See Birkner vs. Lam (2007) 156 Cal.App. 4th 275.) A factual issue remains regarding the application of the litigation privilege. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN S. KARLS VS. MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Quash Service And Dismiss Non-Existing Party
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANT MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, MELLON FINANCIAL SERVICES CORPORATION #1, MELLON LEASING CORPORATION, and, MELLON VENTURES, INC. Motion to Quash Service and Dismiss Non-Existing Party. Motion to quash and dismiss granted. Suit is brought against an entity that does not legally exist. The remaining claims are dismissed with prejudice as to this lawsuit only, as the identical action is brought against these remaining parties in case number 489460. Plaintiff is to proceed with case number 489460. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN S. KARLS VS. MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANT MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, MELLON FINANCIAL SERVICES CORPORATION #1, MELLON LEASING CORPORATION, and, MELLON VENTURES, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer off calendar as moot in view of court's ruling on the motion to dismiss. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN S. KARLS VS. THE BANK OF NEW YORK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2009, line 14, DEFENDANT BNY MELLON PERFORMANCE & RISK ANALYTICS, INC., MELLON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, MELLON FINANCIAL SERVICES CORPORATION #1, MELLON LEASING CORPORATION, MELLON VENTURES, INC., MBSC SECURITIES CORPORATION, URDANG CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC., and, THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. It is unclear that prior court orders bar this action. Plaintiff must plead facts that demonstrate his claim is not preempted under California and federal law and that his conversion claim is for tangible property. (Melchior vs. Newline Productions (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 779.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HELEN MCCLURE VS. MICHAEL GALLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2009, line 16, PLAINTIFF HELEN MCCLURE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether plaintiff accepted rent after the notices were issued. (Gallin declaratin paragraphs 17-18.) Issues for summary adjudication not properly identified. (See CRC 3.1350(b)). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AURORA LOAN SERVICES LLC VS. PETER D BARTOSIK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT PETER BARTOSIK, and, ALEXANDER BARTOSIK DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with five days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege that defendants are former owners or on exemption to the just cause provisions of the rent ordinance. Plaintiffs fail to allege compliance with or exemption from the Rent Ordinance. =(302/CWW)
